name: Acorn
description: Connects local partners and smallholder farmers to carbon markets through agroforestry project support, MRV, certification, and commercialization.

name: PUR
description: Develops agroforestry, restoration, and insetting programs, often linked to corporate supply chains and long-term farmer engagement.

name: re.green
description: Develops large-scale ecological restoration projects with carbon revenue, biodiversity, and native ecosystem recovery at the center of the model.

name: Taking Root
description: Builds farmer-centered reforestation and agroforestry carbon programs with technology, local implementation, and long-term monitoring.

name: Solidaridad
description: Works with producers, supply chains, and landscapes. In carbon projects, often plays the field implementation and farmer engagement role.

name: TechnoServe
description: Provides technical assistance and market systems support. Agroforestry carbon may become a financing layer for farmer livelihoods and resilient supply chains.

name: Catholic Relief Services
description: Potentially relevant as a field implementation and livelihoods partner where carbon can finance restoration, resilience, and farmer support.

name: Verra
description: Operates carbon standards and registry infrastructure used by many voluntary carbon market projects, including nature-based solutions.

name: Gold Standard
description: Certifies climate and sustainable development outcomes, including land-use and nature-based activities where methodology requirements are met.

name: Plan Vivo
description: Focuses on community-led land-use and ecosystem service projects with strong emphasis on local participation and benefit sharing.

name: Pachama
description: Uses remote sensing, software, and data tools to assess and monitor forest carbon projects and connect buyers with nature-based credits.

name: Open Forest Protocol
description: Provides digital monitoring, reporting, and verification infrastructure intended to make forest and restoration projects more transparent and accessible.

name: Chloris Geospatial
description: Uses satellite-based methods to estimate aboveground biomass and help assess forest carbon stocks and change over time.

name: Climate Asset Management
description: Invests in natural capital strategies, including land-based assets where carbon, biodiversity, and sustainable production can form part of the return story.

name: FMO
description: Provides development finance that can support climate, agriculture, and forestry-related investment where projects fit mandate and risk appetite.

name: Microsoft
description: A major corporate buyer whose carbon removal procurement helps shape expectations for quality, durability, monitoring, and delivery risk.

name: Salesforce
description: Corporate buyer and climate actor involved in voluntary carbon market demand, including support for higher-quality nature and removal credits.

name: Nestlé
description: Relevant as a supply-chain actor where agroforestry can connect climate goals, sourcing, farmer resilience, and landscape outcomes.

name: Mars
description: Relevant as a cocoa and agrifood supply-chain company where agroforestry carbon can connect climate, sourcing, and farmer livelihoods.
